title: Profitable Schemes
hints: Notice that the order of crimes does not matter, only which subset is chosen. This suggests building the solution by considering crimes one by one.
This is a variation of the 0/1 Knapsack problem. You need a dynamic programming state that tracks both the total members used and the profit accumulated so far.
To handle the 'at least minProfit' condition efficiently, cap the profit dimension at minProfit. Any profit exceeding this threshold can be treated as exactly minProfit in your state.
